reviews.review_text:
Overall, Gunfire reborn is a very satisfying and addicting roguelite. The progression is nice and seasonal updates change the game frequently. The gunplay is fun and most characters feel balanced. I would recommend getting this game in a bundle with all DLC when its on sale. Most characters are locked behind paid DLC and the game feels pretty lackluster without them.

As many reviews already say, the English translation is rough. Not terrible, but it is difficult to understand exactly what items do and how they interact with other items. Another problem I have with this game is the balancing. Especially in higher difficulties, you are either killing all enemies in 1 hit, or not killing them at all.

I will say this game has scratched that Borderlands itch I've been having. Its fun, Ill probably play more.
This game is pretty damn addicting. The bosses are all super unique and fluid making for a pretty awesome experience. I really love the different class options and ways to play also I like men.

If Risk of Rain 2 and Borderlands had a child but the child turned out to be a furry artist.

Actually really fun and addicting gameplay. Smooth and satisfying reload animations, and flashy combat. Though, movement can feel stiff as your character isn't very fast, and dashing doesn't take you very far either. Nevertheless, if you have an FPS itch and like roguelikes, you might enjoy this.

I can't say enough good things about this game. My playgroup has been playing for 5 years now, Since beta with only 4 characters, all the way back to only two for some of them. We are still playing weekly at most a week break here and there for other games. Its the only online multiplayer that has had this kinda staying power for us, by a longshot.

 Plenty of updates, new levels stages weapons and over doubled the character rosters in that time. They actually meaningful tweak and balance stuff in ways that make the game more fun (If punishing at time, BEWARE the word OPTIMIZED pertaining to enemies in the patch notes. I'm sure more skilled people have quibbles but I can't think of a change that was an outright nerf I was upset about. Just different function.

 The seasonal buff systems have all been fun (IMO). Only one was a slight miss power level wise, but even that is fun to try and optimize. And if you don't like this season's thing you have the option to play any previous season or with none of them, no problem.

 I know the paid character DLCs are a bit of a turn off to new people. One of my few complaints is I wish they would add a more significantly discounted bundle on all of it to ease the burden on players coming in now. However every character in this game has the potential for god like runs (Arguably the Cat and the Dog are still right up there with most broken), and there is plenty to do with he 6 base ones.

The DLCs normally go on sale for a freckle under $5 when new ones come out and I personally haven't minded kicking them that once a year or so with how much I play. But certainly to each their own there.

There is massive variety in builds you can make with all the characters and absolutely broken synergies if you can get the right items. Because of this it is best played with friends so you have a larger pool of scrolls and weapons to trade amongst yourselves.

My only other criticism is we could use a few more difficulty levels. They have expanded this a couple time in the past so I'm sure it will happen again sooner or later. But with four people who know what they are doing it can become a bit of a slaughter in the late game. We do still have a wipe every handful of games so its not terrible by any means, even got got by the final boss a few nights ago. But wiping is part of the fun in roguelites and we would like to be able to crank it a little more.

Gunfire Reborn is one of the best fps roguelites ive ever played. Hundreds of hours of content and builds. My only complaint is the updates. They basically just throw pocket lint at you once every 3 months, making you pay for dlc every 6. In my opinion the newer characters are just dont match up to orginal default characters that come with the game. In terms of the meta progression, you can max everything as soon as a new update drops so the only real new things you can do when an update hits is level up the 2 new characters(which is done with currency that wont even dent your pockets if you've played the game for more than 100 hours), try out the 3 changes to already existing weapons and the seasonal gimics that dont really effect the game that much. For a game that has more than 1 dev and millions of copies sold it really breaks my heart that i can go through a year's worth of updates in less than few hours. Compared to some other games that have 1 dev such as crab champions where every update (completely free btw) it basically changes and improves a lot of fundamental that can lead to more synergies and a lot more ways to play overall. Just to top it all off they dont even allow the coversation of mod support in their discord or steam forums. Like I said, very good game but as a long time fan, I really wish we could get more content out of the dlc's at least.

With a good number of weapons, all of which feel unique in their own way, combined with a decent roster of characters/heroes to play even without DLC, the game can provide challenging runs. However, with enough know-how and the right combinations of level-ups(hero's skills), upgrades(scrolls) and weapons, every run can be successful, and even becomes easier with the seasonal perks.

For players coming from faster-paced FPS or roguelite/roguelike games, it may feel a bit slow, especially movement-wise, although movement can be upgraded. Still, I would recommend it.

Since I haven’t played with bots or tried the online 4-player co-op, I can’t comment on the multiplayer aspect. However, as a single-player experience, it is very good, at least for me.

Very good and fun game, with a very interesting and thoughtful art direction...
Yet, the developers started using genAI in their emoticons, comics and whatnot. It's pretty embarassing especially considering all the care and thought that went in the primordial steps of this game. I've been here for a long time, but it's truly sad seeing this game ending up into the AI slop trap. The use is so blatant and in your face, it's genuinely an insult to whoever bought the game.
Good fun! Plenty of weapon choices, good pace of leveling up.
The developers use generative AI slop for official artwork and emotes. Avoid.

Gunplay is fun and satisfying, hell just look at my playtime. Unfortunately:
- Build variety kind of sucks. Pretty much every character has the exact same 3 builds (weapon, secondary skill, primary skill) with the only differences being what weapon type your character wants to use. After a while it gets VERY stale. Seasonal mechanics are basically identical to each other in terms of buffs and mechanics so the game has next to nothing to shake up the gameplay.

- In-game text is very obviously machine translated. Lots of skill and item/scroll descriptions are borderline nonsensical, with inconsistent terminology and straight-up incorrect descriptions. There is also just a lot of entirely untranslated text that sneaks its way into the game every update.

- DLC power creep is very much a thing. Paid characters are much, much better than base-game characters and it is almost impossible to lose a run with them, even on the highest difficulty.

- Performance and other issues: game has been running worse and worse the longer it's out and this isn't being addressed at all - there has been at least one documented case of a player getting a virus partially due to the game pinging a domain the developers no longer have access to. (potential false-positive, but worth mentioning.)

- UNDISCLOSED GENAI USAGE. GenAI is being used to create both promotional comics seen in the news updates for the game as well as for animated in-game emotes unlocked via the battle pass.
Amazing game if you enjoy rougelites, I had a lot of fun playing as Lyn for the few runs I have done so far

I was genuinely caught off guard by how addictive this game became over the last few months. It’s beautifully chaotic with friends, yet remains a challenging, deeply satisfying solo experience. There are going to be a ton runs where it's a genuine dud due to what the game gives you, and you got to work with it, and sometimes you find some really surprising builds you never would have tried; but nothing beats that dopamine rush where the right scrolls just so happen to fall into your hands and you snowball from struggling from room-to-room to can't be moved. The one thing I would love to see from this game is more area/enemy diversity, because once you go to around reincarnation difficulty you've really seen it and done it all and grows repetitive even with the increased difficulty after every run.
Very fun game with appealing character designs

Game is good but the controller support is terrible.

It only accepts controllers that are recognized through DSX.
My razer Raiju v3 or Scuf envision pro do not work on this game.

I've really enjoyed this game over the years and came back to it again to find my save file was lost, but the devs helped me get back to where I was by compensating me. They care about their community and the game itself, which to me is rare. I strongly recommend you try it out as it's a lot of fun and the damage numbers you can get up to are incredibly high.
very fun fast paced shooter dungeon crawler game, worth the money for shur

Ive enjoyed this game on and off, always feels like a game i can come back to and pull off some random stuff to win it all, some things balance wise seem a bit off but thats every roguelite/roguelike game so its not like surprising but its very very fun and playing with friends is great

Gunfire Reborn is one of those rare games that perfectly mixes FPS, roguelite, and RPG mechanics. Every run feels different thanks to the huge variety of weapons, scrolls, heroes, and builds. Sometimes you're carefully aiming headshots, and ten minutes later you're firing lightning, explosions, and enough projectiles to make the screen look like a fireworks factory.

The progression feels rewarding, the gunplay is satisfying, and the art style has a lot of charm. While it's fun solo, the game truly shines in co-op. Playing with friends and creating ridiculous build combinations leads to some of the most chaotic and memorable runs I've had in any roguelite.

If you enjoy games where every run can turn into an overpowered power fantasy, Gunfire Reborn is an easy recommendation. Just be prepared for the classic "one more run" trap that somehow turns into three hours. 🎮
Fun game! definitely worth giving a try if you like to build your character through power ups and new weapons, the abilities are a very fun addition as well

Very fun and could be overwhelming but trying out different weapons and characters helps make unique builds. The difficulty scaling is great for new players and veteran players. Characters are very cute and skills are cool to use.

Game is really fun.  Tried it for free for a bit on Xbox Play Pass then bought the game here.  A decent mix of rogue-like gameplay with actual skill floors.  That said, there are absolutely broken builds (obviously) and late game grind is more just for the lulz, less for anything.  

No microtransactions, which is great, skins are purchased through earned in-game currency which is not even remotely difficult to farm.  Plenty to do, lots of charm.  Highly reccommend.
Have YOU ever wanted a first-person shooter where you can play as furballs and minmax the absolute hell out of every gun/weapon/aggressive lizard you come across? Then boy do I have a game for you!
this is a great game. i thought it was very fun and a great way to kill time with friends.

good to play animals with your friends. movement can be... intensely frustrating at times bc of how floaty the jumps are combined w no vertical mobility options But the guns feel pretty good and the builds get silly

If you are a fan of borderlands, then this is a game worth considering. 

Co-op focused, and rogue-like driven, this game is alarmingly fun if you got an hour to kill and want to just shoot stuff in a bullet hell. 

I will say that it does get same-y after the first 100 hours, but the dev's clearly care about the game, release updates, new content and items even to this day.

if you got 3 other friends and no idea what to do for a weekend, this is a solid pick if you want a fast paced shooter and not break the bank.

I originally played the game on console so I'd like to start by saying THROW THAT EXPERIENCE OUT OF THE WINDOW. Unlike the console version, the PC version is actively updated and still receives new content and expansions. It's stable fun, there's lots of items and weapons to try, and a lot of silly builds to make. The base game is cheaper than RoR2 right now and the company hasn't shown itself to be absolute scum so honestly... give them your money instead :) ~ it's worth it! Your friend won't buy it? Join me instead, you don't need them 🙏 WHY HAVEN'T YOU BOUGHT IT YET
Once you think you're done with the content, you unlock even more content. The characters are also super fun to play as, the dlc characters even more so.

It took me a while before I decided to review this game, but I highly recommend this game and it's one of my favorite roguelites on steam, even though I may not have as many hours in it as some other games.

The game feels so good, and all the dlc for the game have felt worth the money, as the new characters and weapons are all very fun. I highly recommend this whether you plan on playing solo or with others.

I've played a good 20-30 hours of it on XBOX Game pass, and it's definitely one of my top roguelikes. While not incredibly niche, I feel as though not enough people talk about Gunfire Reborn. The gunplay is great, the characters are all extremely fresh and unique from one another, and overall one of the best 3D shooter roguelikes, on par with Roboquest.
9/10
I would recommend to anyone looking for a good game to fill your time.
An arena rouguelite with friends. Its fun, even if the final boss can like one shot you. After beating it, there is very little reason to return to it. Did not regret getting it though.

Hours of fun but make sure to read reviews on the DLC classes to see which are worth your time if you're interested later. Some of them have negative reviews and the devs don't market them better if there are rebalances for the poorly performing heroes
This game is so addicting and fun.  Don't be a broke ass tho.  Def buy the dlc.  It's worth it.
The first and problably the most solid fps roguelite game out there. Well worth the price especially when on sale
Game is super addicting, very fun solo, even more fun with friends. I'm not a huge rouge-like fan but this one is one even I really enjoy, give it a shot it's great!
This is a phenomenal game. For anyone that loves looter shooters--but feels extremely disappointed in the slop pushed by triple A companies nowadays--give this game a shot!
My new roguelike obsession. Awesome fast-paced gameplay, adorable heroes, satisfying as HELL combat, and a ton of unique and diverse weapons to mow 'em down with. Don't miss out!
Fun roguelike shooter. The constant updates by the devs are amazing. Wish them the best

[h1]Rogue-like Dungeon Shooter?[/h1]
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3330539336

I enjoy a good rogue-like (or-lite) game. I also enjoy shooters - so it was for me a no-brainer to get this game long long long ago as it was in EA state (IF i remember right) but i finally thought about to write a review for this game.

Gunfire reborn (in short GFR) is in its core a really nice rogue-like shooter which has some great guns and gameplay to offer. THOUGH, it suffers from pretty repetitive areas which gets to quick to boring, sadly...

[h1]Great guns, fun characters[/h1]
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3711716192

GFR has a lot weapons and characters to offer which makes the runs feel different to play (at least). Its not just guns but depending if you want or not, you sure can also just go melee or go in mostly with abilites your different characters offer - adding here that the DLCs are also sure worth getting if you enjoy such little things because the DLC weapons AND character are actually well made.

Graficly, it tries to keep it simple but in its own, still fitting style. It reminds me of DRG with an old-school polygon-ish look but still fitting.

THOUGH...
I do have to address that with all the weapons and characters in mind, the game gets way to quick way to boring if you keep the levels in mind, which is kinda sad.

Areas and levels feel sometimes super empty or just long hallways with a curve or similar - and the fact that the first area of the game is literally dark ruins WITH just such said hallways leaves something of a sour taste in the mouth.
The game [b]really does lack different routes or similar...[/b] As i said, the first area are said ruins, than without spoliering "to" much, we have a desert, and THAN we finally see some intresting old-chinese-city like areas plus a small snow area...and than thats that.
They are not really different nor are there said different routes or levels... "yet"...
It was said that such are in the making and i really really hope we get some new level design(s) soon. As i said, the weapons and characters are all nice and most of them never get bored but the levels sadly do. I mean for the most part the first level is like always just a big "L" shaped room, thats that...

[h1]What else can we expect?[/h1]
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3716159432

GFR offers a skilltree with at least 6 characters in tha basegame (IF i remember right). The DLCS add 2 new characters EACH with 4 new weapons as well. I dont now how many weapons the game has "now" but i think there is for sure a weapon for everyone, from bows, snipers to pistols, launchers, flamethrower-like weapons and similar. Even characterwise you have some with element damage, others which go melee and sure some which play totally different as well.

Gunplay is really well made and weapons and characters do feel unique and not just copy/pasted skin changes. Enemies act also very different from each kind. You do have snipers which stand back wile others which run in, some which look for cover and bosses which are sure massive and offer, for the most part, a good fight.
Again, the only thing it lacks as of right now are more, better and different levels. They sadly feels very plain in the design, some can be very short and they dont change a lot - again, its said that will change so i hope it will, for the games sake.

[h1]So do you recommend it?[/h1]
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3599310030

In short, yes even if you might play it for a longer time and have to take a break from it. Its still a lot fun and to come back to for "just another round". Again, Weapons are fun, characters unique. Levels, even if they lack somewhat, are still ok and invite for a nice run.

playing it alone OR with up to 3 more friends, GFR is still an enjoyable game and its nice to see that the devs are still active working on the game as well.

[h1]Recommended. Nice and Unique Characters and Weapons. Very nice Multiplayer-game. It lacks different Levels as of right now but Devs said they work on "that" problem.[/h1]

[h3] Overall for the low price of $20, this game is absolutely worth it for fps/rougelite enjoyers. [/h3]

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[h2] Pros: [/h2]
[list]
    [*] [b] Replayability [/b]
    [*] [b] Simplicity [/b] 
    [*] [b] Weapon Design [/b]
[/list]

With [b] 57 [/b] weapons base game ([b] 67 [/b] with all DLC), [b] 7 [/b] characters ([b] 14 [/b] with all DLC), and [b] 4 [/b] acts, this game has plenty of content to gorge on. Combine that with the huge amount occult scrolls (equivalent to an artifact in other rougelites) and all the unlocks, and time can really fly. There is also solid enemy diversity, you cant just shoot everything (although most things) in the face and expect them to die the same.

[b] I also enjoy how easy the game is to get into [/b], you have a dash, primary and secondary ability, and a few terms like [i] luck shot [/i] (crit chance) and [i] crit X [/i] (crit damage). There are also the 4 different elemental damage types, each being effective/ineffective against certain health bars, but with a little testing and a menu dedicated to such info (the glossary I think its called), they're easy to grasp.

I also have got to mention the characters, The Crown Prince (cat) is very fun to play as he's flexible with what elemental fusions you want to make as his ability applies an element, and also the dog (forgot his name lol) as he played similar to a character from a game I love called Borderlands 2. They all are unique in their own way,

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[h2] Cons: [/h2]
[list]
    [*] [b] Difficulty Scaling [/b]
    [*] [b] DLC content [/b] 
    [*] [b] Translation Issues [/b]
[/list]

The downfalls of this game in my opinion, is how difficulty changes throughout the game, how DLC doesn't add much for its price point, and very slight translation annoyance.

When you start this game fresh, and your skill tree has only a few points in it, the game is quite challenging. Especially when you make the jump from hard to nightmare, it can take a couple of runs to get adjusted to the enemy aggro and damage. But then the game does from fairly challenging to auto pilot with the addition of a new mechanic called [b] spiritual blessings  [/b]

This is only accessible while playing the difficulty after nightmare. Reincarnation (RE for short) has 1-10 levels, each increasing enemy damage/hp (only levels 1-8). [b] spiritual blessings [/b] alter how you play by changing usually how a skill works, and each character has multiple exclusive ones. By changing, I mean just making it absolutely busted (89% of the time). This makes the early game, which is the hardest portion of a run, much much easier. 

The other side affect of this is that by picking a blessing, it is going to dictate how you build your character. Oh you chose a blessing buffing your primary ability? choose all upgrades for your primary ability. [b] Now, with that being said, spiritual blessings are fun [/b], it just gets boring choosing it multiple knowing how your build it going to end up each time.

[b] My other main issue is how DLC is handled [/b]. For [i] $6.99 [/i] buying one of the four DLCs gives you 2 characters and 2 weapons, I wish it was a little more. I'm not going to comment on the characters as fun is subjective, but the fact that there is no new enemies or levels being added doesn't make the game feel as "enhanced". 

I'm still interacting with the same enemies and levels, just with a new weapon and maybe character if I even decide to play them that run. It doesn't feel like its worth the price in my opinion. 

[b] With all that being said, I love this game and do recommend it <3 [/b]

Undisclosed AI art use, in emoticons and promo material from the devs.  Until steam updates the store page with that info the reviews are the only notice.  Keep this as a heads up that the steam ai banner isnt the end all be all for finding it, unfortunately.

The game is lagging randomly and often.
Some of the descriptions are very unclear and the game still gets content, but no one seems to fix these texts.

[h1]Probably the least interesting FPS roguelite I've played + they're selling in-game avatars made with Gen AI[/h1]

Gunfire Reborn's gameplay is very "face-value" and non-dimensional. It lacks movement abilities that other similar games have that would make the game a ton more fun to play. The meta progression is painfully slow yet necessary to get anywhere in a run, making most of your first runs doomed from the start. The buffs you get in a run are either super basic or eggregiously specific like "after giving a fox backshots, receive x% DMG increase if it's a tuesday and his wife was home". 

It doesn't help that the english translation is horrible and item descriptions are literally insane. Overall it really has the feel of an unfinished game with very mid gameplay. I genuinely don't get the Overwhelmingly Positive reviews.

Also don't give me melee weapons when half your boss fights are unhittable 90% of the fight what the hell is this game design bro

Love this game, and while the amount of DLCs is a bit too much, I still love to support indie devs so I don't mind, but latest update BROKE the game.

I CANNOT play, the game uses up all my remaining RAM, pinning my PC at 99% which is ridiculous for a 64gb RAM PC.

Please fix the game. How did this even pass QA?

It's like Hades and Borderlands had a child, and that child is adorable fluffy little animal comitting w@rcrimes left and right.
Utterly adorable, abosolutely fun, and reasonably priced.
Boring, repetitive and long grind to get any new unlocks. Pay to unlock with so many DLC.

134/134 achievements, review time!
over the course of 4+ years, finally did it for the first time after pushing it off for awhile.

8.5/10
the best roguelike fps shooter ive played outa almost a dozen id say. it keeps getting better, more new items, weapons, enchants and seasonal modifiers to really make it even more hectic and fun in harder modes. the devs are great and constantly showcasing things and showing whats to come with a good roadmap and plenty of fixes/updates. unlike some devs that just give up after release. 

idk if some achievements are bugged or its because i did some then stopped playing for awhile and progress just halted. example, 30 elite monster kills, it was stuck at 28/30 and you usually kill dozens of elites in higher modes per floor but it wouldnt pop. so just a heads up, you might have to use SAM to get those that arent going if youve been playing for awhile.  one annoying thing for achievements is every new dlc, adds more so you gotta buy the dlc to 100% again each time but it goes to a great cause if youve spent more then 20 hours in the game, youll see just how much love is here. nothing else to spend money one, all can be earned ingame from challenges/seasonal store so you wont miss anything.

give it a try if you like rouge lits. one of the best out there and just keeps getting better!
You shoot stuff and its fun...
Don't worry guys it ain't a furry game its just a rouge shooter game.
it's like if borderlands was a dungeon crawler and chinese
good fun lots of replay-ability,
starting in the same environment against the same enemies every time is mind numbing

nothing to see here... just the greatest game of all time
